title: Websocket client + advanced subscription never torn down on reconnect/shutdown
description: connectAndRun (cmd/it2ks/main.go:100-146) has no defer c.Close() and no Unsubscribe on exit. runWithBackoff loops; every reconnect attempt allocates a fresh client.Client which spawns a reader goroutine + opens a websocket FD. The old client is orphaned and lives until TCP keepalive expires (minutes) or possibly never. On SIGTERM, the advanced subscription is also never Unsubscribed → iTerm2 keeps emitting to the dead socket until it notices. rg 'c.Close|client.Close' returns 0 hits. Long-running daemon accumulates goroutines + FDs.
acceptance_criteria: connectAndRun has a defer that (a) best-effort Unsubscribes the basic + advanced subs with a short fresh ctx (~2s) and (b) calls c.Close() (or the it2 client's teardown method — verify against library). Reconnect path and shutdown path both exercise the teardown. If it2 lacks a Close, file an upstream issue and document the gap.

title: ResolveApp inherits signal ctx → no timeout, blocks capture loop
description: ResolveApp closure in cmd/it2ks/main.go:128-139 calls c.GetVariable(ctx, sid, 'jobName') with the outer signal ctx that never times out. AppCache.Get (internal/capture/appcache.go:53) invokes the resolver inline on the capture goroutine — a slow or hung iTerm2 jobName lookup blocks the entire keystroke ingestion loop. Notification channel backpressures upstream → the it2 client's ws reader can block → keystrokes dropped or socket killed. Untested.
acceptance_criteria: ResolveApp wraps ctx with context.WithTimeout(ctx, ~1s) (tunable via config). On timeout returns 'unknown'. Paired with negative-caching (separate issue) so timeouts don't refire each event. Test: resolver stub that blocks longer than timeout — capture loop continues processing within ≤ timeout + ε.

title: Reconnect resets session-index → S-collisions in same-day file
description: sessions map, nextIdx, curDate are locals inside capture.Run (internal/capture/capture.go:34-45). runWithBackoff (cmd/it2ks/main.go:73-97) loops connectAndRun on every disconnect; each invocation builds fresh Deps and a fresh Run with nextIdx=0. The writer persists across reconnects, so the same-day .jsonl file accumulates multiple distinct {type:session, s:0, ...} headers with different sid/app. Downstream replay indexed by S misattributes events. Untested.
acceptance_criteria: sessions/nextIdx/curDate lifted out of Run into a struct owned by main (or passed via Deps) so reconnects reuse the table. Test: two sequential Run calls into the same recording writer with distinct sids — assert no duplicate s index across the combined output.

title: Dual-clock midnight race: capture and writer recompute date independently
description: capture.Run computes the day-rotation date with d.Now() at internal/capture/capture.go:59-79; writer.Write recomputes with w.now() at internal/writer/writer.go:35. When the two reads straddle midnight UTC, a SessionRecord can land in day N while the corresponding event line lands in day N+1 — orphan event with no header in N+1. Worse: capture may reset its session-index map on day N+1 and assign s:0 to a different sid/app while a stray pre-rollover event with s:0 still references the prior session inside day N+1's file. Violates the doc-stated 'each day's file is self-contained' invariant. Untested path.
acceptance_criteria: writer.Write accepts an explicit time.Time (or formatted date) from the caller; only one clock read per event. Test: inject a clock that ticks across midnight between capture's read and writer's write — assert header+event land in the same file and no index collision.

title: Implement keystroke capture MVP
description: Build the Go daemon: config loading, event type, JSONL writer, app cache, subscription, handler, main entry point. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-implementation.md
close_reason: MVP shipped in commits 0abf730..fb9637a; umbrella commit forgot to flip bd state

title: No backpressure on writer/resolver → slow disk blocks ws reader
description: The notification channel from it2 client → capture.Run → cache.Get → writer.Write is a single synchronous pipeline. If writer.Write blocks (fsync stall, disk pressure) or ResolveApp blocks (F3), the capture loop stops draining d.Notifications, the it2 client's reader goroutine blocks on send, and iTerm2 may classify as slow consumer and drop the subscription. No drop-oldest policy, no select-with-default, no measured channel buffer.
acceptance_criteria: Decouple via a buffered intermediate channel with documented capacity and drop-oldest policy on overflow (or drop-newest with a counter). Capture loop pulls from ws, enqueues; a separate writer goroutine drains. Metric: count of dropped events. Test: writer that blocks ≥1s — verify capture loop drains the source channel and counts drops.

title: Flush goroutine not joined; post-Close writer reopen path
description: cmd/it2ks/main.go:42-58: flush ticker goroutine started without WaitGroup/done-chan. On shutdown, ctx cancel → runWithBackoff returns → defer w.Close() runs concurrently with the flusher's exit. Mutex serializes calls and the nil-guard in Flush masks the post-Close race, but the structure is fragile. Sharper concern: writer.Write has no closed-state flag — if a stray notification is processed after w.Close (e.g., select picked the notification branch before noticing ctx.Done), Write will rotate and reopen the file at line 35-40 via rotateLocked since curDate is now ''; that reopened file is never closed/flushed and data dies in OS buffer.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Join the flusher via done-chan before defer w.Close(). (2) Add a closed-flag field to Writer; Write returns ErrClosed instead of reopening. Tests: shutdown race test using a synchronous clock + injected notification.

title: AppCache: resolver errors not negatively cached → ws storm on dead sessions
description: internal/capture/appcache.go:53-56: on resolver error returns 'unknown' without writing to c.entries. Every keystroke from a session whose GetVariable fails (SESSION_NOT_FOUND race between close + buffered notification, or transient ws error) triggers a fresh websocket round-trip. Combined with F3 (no resolver timeout) and F8 (Filter doesn't drop 'unknown' apps), bursts on closing sessions block the capture loop. Note: appcache.go:11 doc claims default TTL 30s but capture.go:35 uses 5s — stale comment.
acceptance_criteria: Negative-cache errors with short TTL (1-2s): on error, write entries[sid]=appEntry{app:'unknown', fetchedAt:now}. Fix the TTL comment to match capture.go's 5s. Test: 10 Gets on erroring session within 1s → resolver called ≤ 2 times.

title: Filter does not normalize case/whitespace → silent app filter miss
description: internal/capture/filter.go:11-22 keys the map on the raw config string. iTerm2 jobName returns lowercase process names (zsh, vim); config TOML may contain '1Password' or ' zsh ' which never match. Given IncludeChars=true default, a user adding apps_exclude = ['1Password'] expects keystrokes filtered but gets them captured — privacy regression.
acceptance_criteria: Normalize on load and at lookup: strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(s)). Test: filter with 'Vim' in apps_exclude blocks app='vim'. Document case-insensitive match in config example.

title: Backoff: never resets after long success; loops forever on permanent subscribe error
description: cmd/it2ks/main.go:68-97. (a) backoff doubles to 60s during initial connect failures, then is never reset on success. After hours-long stable connection drops, next reconnect waits up to 60s → 60s of keystroke loss. (b) sendAdvancedKeystrokeSubscribe non-OK status (main.go:172-176) returns error that runWithBackoff retries indefinitely with no escalation — a permanent API rejection (future iTerm2 change) silently spins forever, daemon captures nothing, user only sees launchd stderr.
acceptance_criteria: Reset backoff to 1s when connectAndRun ran ≥30s before erroring. Classify permanent vs transient subscribe-failure: permanent → log.Fatalf (launchd surfaces it) or exit non-zero after N attempts.

title: Failed SessionRecord write still commits index → orphan events on partial writer failure
description: internal/capture/capture.go:69-79 inserts into sessions map (sessions[key]=idx; nextIdx++) BEFORE marshaling and writing the SessionRecord. If d.Writer.Write fails (ENOSPC, EIO, transient disk hiccup) the error is logged and the loop continues — but the index bookkeeping is already committed. Subsequent events for that sid succeed and reference an s:N whose header never landed in the file. Day's file has events pointing at non-existent headers.
acceptance_criteria: Reorder: marshal + Write the header first; only on success update sessions/nextIdx. On failure, leave the map untouched so the next event retries the header. Fault-injection test: writer returns error on first header write, success thereafter — assert second event for same sid carries a fresh header.

title: Log files written 0o644 with IncludeChars=true default → keystrokes (incl. secrets) world-readable
description: internal/writer/writer.go:72 opens day file with mode 0o644. internal/writer/writer.go:24 creates ~/.it2ks with 0o755. internal/config/config.go:64 defaults IncludeChars=true → raw typed characters (potentially passwords typed into unfiltered terminals) are recorded. On multi-user macOS or any local privilege escalation, /Users/dk/.it2ks/logs/*.jsonl is readable by other accounts.
acceptance_criteria: Open files with 0o600, mkdir log dir + ~/.it2ks with 0o700. Flip IncludeChars default to false (opt-in). Test asserts os.Stat(file).Mode().Perm() == 0o600.

title: Resolve iTerm2 API magic-cookie auth before smoke test
description: First run of `./it2ks` triggered iTerm2's Python API trust dialog demanding a root-owned magic file (hex-encoded binary path + UUID 61DF88DC-3423-4823-B725-22570E01C027). Dialog path not captured. Two paths forward: (a) re-trigger dialog, capture the file path it names, create file via sudo tee with correct contents+ownership; (b) iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect' (bypass cookie, looser but fine on single-user machine). Blocks it2ks-tya (advanced=true verification) and it2ks-p3f (Task 11 smoke test) — both need the daemon to actually connect.
close_reason: Bypassed via iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect' (acceptable on single-user machine). Follow-up filed as it2ks-ls3 to revisit proper file-based trust.

title: Verify iTerm2 honors advanced=true via re-subscribe pattern
description: cmd/it2ks/main.go sends a basic keystroke subscribe via SubscribeToGenericNotifications, then immediately re-subscribes with KeystrokeMonitorRequest{Advanced:true}. Unverified whether iTerm2 actually upgrades the existing subscription on the second request, or whether ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED means the body was ignored. Confirm empirically by observing 'up' events in the log; if absent, may need to unsubscribe-then-subscribe-advanced instead.
close_reason: Fixed via 433c05b. SubscribeToGenericNotifications was passing empty session string, which iTerm2 rejects with SESSION_NOT_FOUND. Now uses 'all'. Also: basic subscribe → unsubscribe → advanced subscribe so iTerm2 doesn't return ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED and drop the advanced flag. Verified locally: log shows both 'down' and 'up' events. Bonus fix: JSON-decode iTerm2's variable returns so app tag is 'node' not '"node"'.

title: it2ks manual integration smoke test (Task 11)
description: Run ./it2ks foreground, type in iTerm2 sessions, inspect ~/.it2ks/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl, validate with scripts/validate-log.go, verify 'up' events appear (advanced=true works), verify app tagging differs per session. Then make install + verify launchd. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-keystroke-capture.md Task 11.
notes:
Partial verification complete (2026-05-28):
- ./it2ks foreground: connects, no errors, writes to ~/.it2ks/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl ✓
- 'up' events present (advanced=true honored after re-subscribe fix) ✓
- scripts/validate-log.go: 'checked N events, 0 errors' ✓
- App tags work (e.g. 'node' — JSON quotes now stripped) ✓
- make install: launchd loads, PID active, no stderr errors ✓
Still needs dk to: type into multiple iTerm2 sessions (different jobs e.g. vim/zsh), verify per-session 'app' differs in the log, confirm no perf impact.

title: Implement it2ks MVP keystroke capture
description: Track end-to-end build per docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-27-it2ks-keystroke-capture.md. 11 tasks: bootstrap, config, event, filter, appcache, writer, capture loop, main wiring, validator, launchd, smoke test.
close_reason: MVP code complete: tasks 1-10. 13 unit tests pass. Review fixes applied (single time.Now per event, robust closeLocked, AppCache concurrency docs). Task 11 manual smoke moved to its own issue.

title: No parent-dir fsync after daily rotation creates new file
description: internal/writer/writer.go rotateLocked closes the old file (flush+Sync+Close) then opens the new dated file but never fsyncs the parent dir. On power loss within seconds of rotation, the new day's file inode may exist but the directory entry not yet committed → file unrecoverable. APFS makes this less likely than ext4 but not impossible.
acceptance_criteria: After os.OpenFile for the new dated path, open parent dir and Sync(). Alternative: document the risk in the writer godoc and accept (single-user local dev tool).

title: Empty jobName success cached as app="" → ambiguous downstream records
description: ResolveApp closure (cmd/it2ks/main.go:128-139): when c.GetVariable returns ("", nil) — e.g., session opened before its child process spawned — the empty string is returned with no error, AppCache stores it, downstream session records carry app:"". Filter.Allow("") is true under default filter so the event is logged; downstream may not handle empty app correctly.
acceptance_criteria: In ResolveApp, if unquoted == "" treat as error or return "unknown". Test: stub GetVariable returning ("",nil) → AppCache caches "unknown" not "".

title: expandHome only handles "~/" prefix → silent wrong-dir writes
description: internal/config/config.go:69-75 only expands paths starting with "~/". A config containing log_dir = "~" or "~user/logs" passes through verbatim; os.MkdirAll then creates a literal "~" directory in the daemon CWD (typically / under launchd). User silently loses logs to wrong location.
acceptance_criteria: Detect any leading "~" and error if not "~/". Test: log_dir="~" returns config validation error; "~user/" rejected with clear message.

title: AppCache: unbounded map growth (no eviction)
description: internal/capture/appcache.go: entries added on every successful Get, never deleted. TTL only triggers refresh, not removal. Long-running daemon across many ephemeral iTerm sessions (tmux churn, repeated tab open/close) accumulates entries indefinitely. ~80 bytes per entry × thousands → slow leak, not catastrophic but unbounded.
acceptance_criteria: Lazy eviction: on Get, drop entries older than TTL × N (e.g., 10×). Or add Forget(sid) called when iTerm session-end notification observed. Test: 1000 Gets on distinct stale sids → map size bounded.

title: Revisit iTerm2 magic-cookie auth (proper file-based trust)
description: Follow-up to it2ks-8hq. For MVP we bypassed via iTerm2 Prefs → General → Magic → 'Allow all apps to connect'. That's fine on dk's single-user machine, but the proper path is: re-trigger the trust dialog, capture the magic-file path it names, create the file via sudo tee with correct contents+ownership (UUID 61DF88DC-3423-4823-B725-22570E01C027 + hex-encoded binary path). Do this before any multi-user or distribution scenario.
